aliases: Coffee with evaporated milk no sugar

english: Coffee with evaporated milk no sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'coffee beverage' class; no specific entry for Kopi C Kosong (Singapore-style coffee with evaporated milk, unsweetened). Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers prepared coffee broadly; no specific facet for evaporated-milk addition or unsweetened variant. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 33352006 covers coffee as a substance; no specific concept for Kopi C Kosong preparation style.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of Kopi C Kosong is approximately 250 g, as typically served at a Singapore kopitiam.

nutrient_source.title: Kopi C (Coffee with Evaporated Milk, No Sugar) — Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Kopi C Kosong (cr_id D09010037). Micronutrients (calcium, iron, cholesterol, saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C) were present in the input record attributed to a secondary source; these have been retained with confidence 0.70 and are cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entry for evaporated milk (FDC ID 170877) for plausibility. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB or the secondary source are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for coffee with milk (unsweetened)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kopi C Kosong specifically. GI estimated at 39 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for unsweetened coffee with milk (whole milk analogue), which reports GI ~39–45. Evaporated milk has a GI of approximately 30–34 (lactose-based); the blended beverage GI is dominated by the low-GI lactose contribution. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (39 × 10.6) / 100 = 4.1 g. This is a low GL. The input record cited GI 55 (medium) from an 'Embedded GI Database' which is not an approved source; that value has been replaced with this approved-source analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (26 mg/100 g) and no added sugar; caffeine content warrants moderation
hypertension.guidance: Kopi C Kosong is suitable for individuals with hypertension when limited to 1–2 cups per day, as its sodium content is negligible (26 mg/100 g) and it contains no added sugar; however, Robusta coffee beans deliver higher caffeine than Arabica, and caffeine intake exceeding 300 mg/day can acutely elevate blood pressure, so daily cup count should be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (4.24 g/100 g), no added sugar, low GI (~39), low glycemic load per serving (~4.1)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kopi C Kosong is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes because it contains no added sugar, has a low glycemic index (~39) and a low glycemic load per standard 250 g cup (~4.1 g), with the 1.78 g/100 g sugar being naturally occurring lactose from evaporated milk; individuals with very tight glucose targets should account for the total carbohydrate (~10.6 g per cup) within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.6 g/100 g), low cholesterol (4 mg/100 g), no added sugar, and coffee polyphenols with documented cardioprotective associations
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kopi C Kosong poses a low cardiovascular risk profile due to its low saturated fat (0.6 g/100 g), low cholesterol (4 mg/100 g), and negligible trans fat (0.04 g/100 g); moderate habitual coffee consumption (1–3 cups/day) is associated with reduced cardiovascular event risk in epidemiological literature, though individuals with arrhythmia or caffeine sensitivity should limit intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Coffee polyphenols (chlorogenic acids) with anti-inflammatory properties; no added sugar or significant pro-inflammatory fats
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Kopi C Kosong is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as it provides anti-inflammatory coffee polyphenols, contains no added sugar, and has low saturated fat (0.6 g/100 g); those with dairy sensitivity should monitor individual response to the evaporated milk component, and intake should be limited to 2 cups/day to avoid caffeine-related sleep disruption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kopi C Kosong is a traditional Singapore kopitiam (coffee shop) beverage consisting of locally roasted Robusta coffee brewed strong and mixed with evaporated milk, served without any added sugar. The name derives from the Hainanese-influenced Singaporean coffee lexicon: 'Kopi' means coffee, 'C' denotes evaporated milk (as opposed to condensed milk used in standard 'Kopi'), and 'Kosong' means empty or zero — signifying no sugar. The drink has a rich, slightly bitter, roasted coffee flavour with a creamy, mildly sweet note from the lactose naturally present in evaporated milk. It is a staple breakfast and morning beverage across Singapore's multicultural community, consumed by Chinese, Malay, Indian, and other communities at kopitiams island-wide. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 39 kcal, 1.3 g protein, 1.9 g fat (0.6 g saturated), 4.24 g carbohydrate (1.78 g sugar from lactose), 0.32 g dietary fibre, and 26 mg sodium. A standard 250 g cup delivers approximately 97.5 kcal and 10.6 g carbohydrate. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 39 (low), yielding a glycemic load of ~4.1 per serving. It is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed in moderation (1–2 cups/day), with the primary caution being caffeine content from Robusta beans.
common_user_queries: Is Kopi C Kosong suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in a cup of Kopi C Kosong?
What is the difference between Kopi C and Kopi C Kosong?
Is Kopi C Kosong low sugar?
Can I drink Kopi C Kosong if I have high blood pressure?
What milk is used in Kopi C?
How much caffeine is in Kopi C Kosong?
What does kosong mean in coffee?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09010037, confidence 0.95). Micronutrients cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central evaporated milk entry for plausibility. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for unsweetened coffee with milk; original input GI source was not approved and has been replaced. All mandatory multilingual names present. Ontology codes are defensible close matches with documented gap notes.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for Kopi C Kosong (D09010037) nor in the secondary micronutrient source used in the input record; detailed fatty acid fractions and remaining micronutrient data for this traditional beverage preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or HPB as of 2026. 2. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB source; coffee and evaporated milk do not contribute meaningful vitamin C. 3. GI value of 55 (medium) from the input record's 'Embedded GI Database' was rejected as a forbidden source; replaced with analogous estimate of 39 (low) from University of Sydney GI Database entry for unsweetened coffee with milk. 4. Calcium (36 mg/100 g), iron (0.04 mg/100 g), cholesterol (4 mg/100 g), saturated fat (0.6 g/100 g), trans fat (0.04 mg/100 g) were attributed to a secondary source in the input; these values are plausible and consistent with USDA FoodData Central evaporated milk data and have been retained at confidence 0.70. 5. The 'keto' dietary tag from the input has been retained in classification dietary flags as the beverage is low-carbohydrate (4.24 g/100 g) and contains no added sugar, making it compatible with ketogenic dietary patterns in typical serving sizes.
